tweets.text:
"With B20 stablecoins we're moving towards a place where we can do 20K transactions per second by the end of the year. Right now it's more like 6K to 7K pre-B20"

Jesse on Base's Azul, Beryl and Cobalt upgrades and what each one changes

"One thing we did over the last six months is we moved Base onto its own dedicated stack. Historically we used a third-party stack from Optimism, but as we've continued to scale, having more control of the stack lets us deliver a better product experience. Azul was the first upgrade where we moved onto it, Beryl was the second, Cobalt is the third, coming late August or September. We're trying to get on a fast cadence, shipping every 6 to 12 weeks instead of waiting a year"

"With Beryl we launched the B20, a tokenization standard that lets issuers, whether they're issuing stablecoins or tokenized equities or any asset, use a trusted standard to get a ton out of the box. That gives us more controls, compliance, privacy, and because it's a native token standard it makes it much faster and much cheaper. We've already done something like $150M of volume of people trading the B20 asset"

"With Cobalt the focus is on native accounts, making it so when folks set up new accounts on Base it just works better. They can pay for gas in any stablecoin, not have to do multiple approvals, it's easier to do key rotation, more controls around multi-sig setup. Then the big focus for the chain is making it work better for trading, rolling out things that enable traders and folks building exchanges, financing and lending to build better products"

• 2025-08-20: 25.708M ZRO → $49.36M at $1.92 → 17.16% of market cap.
• 2025-09-20: 25.708M ZRO → $50.65M at $1.97 → 17.61% of market cap.
• 2025-10-20: 25.708M ZRO → $43.70M at $1.70 → 15.19% of market cap.

And now ZRO is trading at $0.7.

tweets.text:
Real Yields operate with cycles or regimes.

During the "money printing" era from 2009 to 2022 (red), the 10-year real yield averaged just 0.23%. We are not in a zero-yield, money-printing era anymore (thank god).

Before this (left blue), the 10-year real yield averaged 2.74%. I have argued the current period (right blue) should look like the pre-money-printing era (left blue), and it does. It also means 10-year real yields can go much higher before they become too restrictive (probably above 3%).

What would cause 10-year real yields to go higher?  Sticky inflation (we have now) and a Fed that does not "choose" (as Warsh terms it) to act on it.
@Marcomadness2

tweets.text:
Personally, I am aggressively buying while most are maximum bearish right now because tokens are trading at all time lows and every chart looks dead.

Meanwhile we’re on calls with those exact same teams every week, and the picture behind the scenes couldn’t be more different. Many of them are quietly doing multi-million dollar revenues right now. Most of the market has no idea, simply because these things take years to build and none of it shows up on a chart until it does.

What almost nobody is pricing in: their pipelines are filling up.

Partnerships, integrations, product launches, enterprise deals. Founders are deliberately sitting on announcements right now because they know news gets absorbed and forgotten in a market like this. So they’re stacking it and waiting.

The moment sentiment turns even slightly, all of that comes out at once. Every team firing their news cascade into a market that’s finally paying attention again. That’s how you get reflexivity that catches everyone off guard.

This is exactly why we’re aggressively buying the most unobvious opportunities we can find while everyone else is midcurving, complaining, and giving up.

The setup is right in front of you. Most are just too exhausted to look at it.

tweets.text:
Just learnt that Hermes might just be amazing at Oil Trading after all
​
4 Wins with ~63% ROI (albeit with a small bankroll)
​
Ran an experiment that lets Hermes auto-trade on Oil Daily Up Down market
​
Hermes started with $200 bankroll, leverages Synthdata daily forecast oil signals, and executes & settle via BlockRun x402
​
If Synth agrees with the market (positive delta) by ≥ 2 percentage point / disagrees with the market (negative delta) by ≤ 8 percentage point, Hermes executes the trade
​
Might just be luck during US-Iran escalation period but excited to see how Hermes performs over the next few months
​
(BlockRun comes in handy because it helps get over Polymarket Geoblock that users might face with CLOB API)
